So how to double your webinar sales guaranteed webinar hack number one. I basically, I run a webinar every week. Mostly it's an affiliate offer. Sometimes it's an offer of my own. I've a list of about 20,000 people who love getting on webinars. That didn't just happen. I kind of, I've trained, been training them for several years. And I close it out at the end of the weekend, Sunday at midnight. Webinar hack number two. Oh, let me just go back. Right. So as, as Russell mentioned, it's basically three days pushing into a webinar, a live event on Thursday and then Friday, Saturday, Sunday is pretty much various different formats and iterations of the replay. Webinar hack number two, you may be saying, Hey, so I have no idea how to promote my webinar. Well, just take the, the, the perfect webinar template. If it's your webinar and you've got the headline there, right? That's going to be the main, the main headline of the, you know, your first slide of the webinar just says Russell laid it out. But if you're promoting an affiliate webinar like I do, you may be saying, wow, how do I, how do I do it? What I recommend to you is to know your audience, know your subscribers, know who they are and what they're looking for in the first place. And if you don't know how to do that, it's pretty easy. Just put together a little, what do they call it? Survey monkey. And just send it out to them. It's like, Hey, how can I help you? What are you most interested in? And then say, you know, SEO, if you're in the I am market, like I am, Kindle publishing, product creation, traffic, whatever. And you'll, you'll know pretty, pretty quickly what they're most interested in, the value that you can provide to them. And I reverse engineer all of my webinar campaigns based upon what I know or what I think I know about most of my subscribers. Um, I built my subscribers from, uh, I know for a fact that they're crazy about Amazon. Um, I, I went through a period of time where I was launching a low ticket product. Didn't know warrior form, WSO, stuff like that. Okay. I was sort of the class of 2011. And after my first, um, I launched my first, uh, information products. It was how to Kindle publishing was basically a case study of a success story that I had and I got like WSO the day and that generated like almost a thousand sales, like organically between, um, that Mike Lance promoting it and other affiliates promoting and then I was hooked and I made a, uh, an agreement with myself that I was going to promote, I was going to launch a new product every week, come up with the product idea, uh, create the product itself. I usually do over the shoulder videos, whatever, and write a sales page and launch it every week. And so that's how I built my list. That's kind of crazy. I don't know how I did that, uh, at this point in time when I look back on it, but, um, yeah, whatever you need to do, right, to build your audience, if you want to take the, uh, the model that I am right now, it's a lot easier with Facebook. I mean, Facebook ad ads were not around at that point in time. Um, so yeah, if you're wondering where I get my traffic from, that's most of them. And so as a result of, uh, most of those folks, they're mostly interested in Amazon publishing, Amazon physical products, Amazon affiliate program, all things Amazon, my list is big enough now and diverse enough so that, uh, I can pretty much promote anything to them at this point, but I still like to bring it back to, uh, something that I, I pretty much know they're going to be interested in or most folks are going to be interested in. For example, I had a, um, I recently promoted a webinar that was a press release service and I was like, wow, how am I going to, how, okay. So what can I do? I came up with two hooks. Webinar hack number four, uh, this is really cool. This is a, um, you've probably seen this. If you, if you've registered for a good webinar event, 24 hours afterwards, they send out an automated message to everybody who's registered for the events and the default is like, thanks for attending or something. Don't think dumb. Why not get, go to webinars to promote your replay by putting an actual URL in that thank you message and say, Hey, thanks for signing up for the webinar. Um, if you would like to watch the replay, you can do it right here for the next 48 hours or so. Uh, and I just plop the URL that I'm going to post the replay page to after the webinar, but again, you have to set up before the webinar because you can't do it retroactively after it's closed down. So yeah, just set that, just come up with a URL and that's where you're going to upload the replay page to, and you get a free mailing from go to webinar. How cool is that? Webinar hack number five, again, prior to the webinar, this is where the rubber hits the road and this is where you get to test out the, uh, your hooks or, um, what you thought, you know, your folks are going to like and you're going to be able to, uh, to kind of test that out simply by, um, loading it up as your subject line, even, I mean, really these emails come together really easily. You can just go one, two, three bullets. You can have a very close match to your email copy and your registration page. In fact, I recommend that you do. And if you send out a, uh, an email and you get tons of opens and clicks and it's different than the hook that you came up with initially that you thought folks were going to, it was going to resonate with your folks, then, uh, you know, you might think about changing the copy on your registration page so that there's a message match between your email and your registration page and you're going to be increasing conversions. Does that make sense? Okay, here's my trial close. So, um, but the thing is, right? So if you start mailing, uh, if you start promoting on Tuesday and my webinars on Thursday evening, I've got Tuesday morning, Tuesday evening, Wednesday morning, Wednesday evening, and then another one on Thursday morning. So four or five chances to start testing different hooks that might resonate with, with, uh, your, your subscribers that may be different than, uh, what you originally thought they would respond to. And once you identify something, you're going to, that's going to want to be reflected again in your registration page in your, uh, you can set it up as the first slide of the webinar, right? Why not? So that there's continuity right there and folks are like, oh, I'm in the right place. Okay, great. I found my, my writer that does this for me on Odesk and I pay her like 75 bucks or a hundred bucks and she gives me back, um, anywhere between like five to 10,000 words and it's a summary of the presentation. In other words, she's just sitting down and she's clicking play and she's listening to whatever 60 seconds or however long she does it. And then just summarizing it's not a transcription. Oh, and I call these whatever PDF guides, PDF summaries, cliff notes. Um, what have you? This is one that I did for Russell. Just to give you an example of what it looks like. And I like to see, I tell her, you know, get, get a screenshot on the, uh, on at least every other page. So it keeps the pages turning and folks like feel like they're, they're not missing out if they missed the presentation, if they're able to see, you know, some of the slides in it as well. Webinar hack number nine, uh, this seems kind of dumb. I put this in here and like, oh, this is so commonsensical. I'm not going to do it, but this is really important. You go through the presentation. You go through the offer and the pitch and you dropped a link in the chat box and the link's not working. It takes people to, uh, maybe the servers down, maybe it's a four or four page. Who knows, right? Uh, but the point is test the link. This is money time. And, um, this is what you've been working up to all week. Uh, just make sure that you test. This is so easy to do, but I can't tell you this happened to me a couple of times and it's always, I don't know how this works. It always seems to happen when I haven't tested the link. So, uh, just test the link beforehand. Even if it's your own offer, just make sure that you're, you know, host gator is still up or whatever it is. Webinar hack number 10. Okay. I mean, these are your, your hottest buyers, the folks who are, um, telling you the, uh, the objections. Webinar hack number 12, this is when the bonus page comes into play after the pitch. These are brand new customers explaining exactly why they jumped in. Maybe it's a business that they want to, you know, work with their, their daughter, uh, who just lost her job. Maybe, maybe this is part of their retirement plan. Who knows, right? But this is how these third party endorsements are golden because people recognize themselves in their own situation in it. And so I think all of, uh, all of my Facebook buyers, uh, all the testimonials that I got, I think are showing up in, in his documents, but this is where you put them, add them to the end of this documents and just title, make it a new chapter and, uh, in your table of contents saying what people are saying about XYZ offer. Sometimes I have a dozen pages of these testimonials, um, that people are, are viewing when they, when they open up this PDF document on Sunday. Dot, dot, dot. Webinar hack number 15. I didn't, I never used to do this. Actually, I started, I used to, uh, again, I've got a Thursday night webinar. And I, uh, typically just used to mail into the replay the next day, but about eight weeks, eight months ago, I started, uh, creating what I call live on core. And this is nothing but a brand new go to webinar event that I populate from the registrants who signed up for Thursday. I just download them inside a go to webinars called the, uh, registration report and force them into a new go to webinar events with an app called Zapier, which is pretty cool. And, um, so I've got a brand new live event set up for Friday for folks who weren't able to make it Thursday night, or maybe they made it Thursday night. They want to watch it again. I give them that opportunity, but it's not a live event. It's basically a recording, like a live replay of the Thursday night events. I don't pretend that it's live. Um, I think lots of folks at this point know lots of my subscribers know that it's not live, but you know, I'm behind the scenes, my team's behind the scenes and they're answering questions just like it was a live event. So for all intents and purposes, it is, I just doesn't require the presenter to be there. And this is ad, this adds like 20 to 25% of sales. You'd be surprised like how many people show up on a Friday afternoon at 2pm Eastern time, which is the time that I usually do them. I don't know what people are usually doing on Friday afternoon, but maybe they're playing hooky or maybe they're in their cubicle, you know, their headset on and watching the, um, watching this live encore, but I get like hundreds of people tuning in and buying on just a recording, uh, played live the next day. And I don't know how to do this, by the way. I'm sorry. I can't tell you, uh, I'm fairly low tech. I have a guy. I got a guy. His name is Eli. He's a, um, I call him a superstar webinar, uh, video guy. And he records them on Thursday night. He takes care of the live replay. Uh, he tells me that I asked him once like, so how do you do this in case I have to do this? You know, I still wouldn't be able to do it. How do you do it? How do you do these live on course? Apparently it involves a little audio patch cable like, uh, like this one that you see there, but I recommend that you don't, that you don't try it. You should just be focusing for the same reason that I have my team on to answer all the questions behind the scenes so that I'm not doing that. I recommend that you just hire, hire Eli. It's a hundred bucks per, um, per event. It's the best hundred bucks you'll ever spend and all that you need to do is focus on the marketing. Web and our hack number 16. Okay. So we talked about the live, uh, the live event on Thursday night, the live encore on Friday afternoon, Saturday morning, I send out a replay page. I just set it up with a template ties, click funnels, replay page, make sure you put a timer on the top, counting down to when the webinar offer is going to expire. In my case, it's a midnight on Sunday, Pacific time. Saturday is usually a slow day for sales, you know, between Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, that's usually my slowest day. Uh, but I still get hundreds and hundreds of clicks of folks watching the replay that didn't make the live event. Um, I mail out, I blast Saturday morning, Saturday evening, and I get that free, uh, that free replay blast. We go to webinar, like I told you 24 hours after the live event after both live events, by the way, and Sunday. I send out that PDF and call it cliff notes. We call it a PDF cheat sheet, whatever you want to call it. Um, I just saved the word document as a PDF and load it up. I think I load it up as an S three link and I post it to my replay page. So folks are clicking through the email to my replay page where I've got a download. I've got the video replay and I also have a download of the PDF. And, um, these drive so many clicks. This is my secret weapon. If I have one, this is what Russell was talking about on the last day. Sometimes I get, you know, 5,000 opens and 2,000 clicks for a list of 20,000 people just for this PDF. I mean, it's crazy. People go, and they're so thankful that I took the time. I mean, no one else, I don't know too many other people that are doing this, but, but now you can start doing it and people will be so appreciative. They're like, Oh my gosh, thank you so much for, for doing this because these are people that either didn't, um, either missed the webinar on Thursday, missed the live encore on Friday, could care less about, you know, watching a two hour video replay on Saturday, but they're more than happy to click on a link and download a PDF of that very same information. Uh, and consume it that way. It's a different, it's a different learning modality. And I know my folks are, are super text based and so am I. So, I mean, that's what I'd be doing. I wouldn't be watching a replay. I would not. I didn't, I don't watch webinars as a rule. Yeah, I know. So, uh, make sure that you, that you've got a live, um, hyperlink in the PDF. I, um, I sprinkle it all the way throughout after the, uh, after my, my offer as it shows up in the content of the summary and I also add it as a footer. Put that link, put it live link and make sure you test that live link again after you save it as a PDF because sometimes it doesn't work. Put it as the footer. So that shows up on every page. So whatever they are in the presentation, when they're ready to buy or check out the, uh, check out the, um, the offer page. They can just click through from the footer there. All right. Last but not least webinar hack number 17. Uh, we're closing it out. This is time to drop the hammer. It's Sunday night. This is when I make so many sales, uh, because my subscribers know that these are time sensitive and come Monday, even if they wanted to buy it, it's not going to be there, no exceptions. And you have to be firm on that too. Um, you're not going to get massive action on Sunday night. If you are, you know, if you're playing loose on whether you're going to, it's going to be available on Monday. So my subscribers know that it's gone on, on Monday. This is last chance. And my last call email usually goes out five hours before. Whoops. Sorry. Usually goes five, goes out five hours before the, um, the timer's counting down. It's about five or six hours before. And it's short and sweet. I'm not doing any selling. I've already been doing, I've been selling this thing all week. This is just for the, for the people who, uh, have been procrastinating who, um, who knew that they wanted to buy it on Thursday, but they had to check with their, their husband or significant other. They had to get their money together, whatever, whatever it is. Okay. It's short and sweet. I usually start it with final notice or last chance or, um, you know, closing in six hours and it's just says, Hey, just a reminder, this is going to be gone in five hours. Here's the link. Have a great week. And I make so many sales on Sunday night. I make a Russell told me he freaked out when he saw how many sales he made on the last, in the last four or five hours, um, just following this, uh, this Sunday temp, uh, system mail out three times on Sunday. This is the last one. And that link, by the way, is not taking them to the replay page. I don't want any leakage. I want them to go directly to the order page. So whatever they can look it up if they want the PDF or, or to watch the replay, but this is taking them right to the order page because these are people that are ready to buy. And if it's not, they're not clicking on it. You know, they're not opening it up on Sunday night. Okay. There you go. That was the distillation of about four or five years worth of, uh, what I've been doing on webinars to optimize my sales, uh, my clicks, my registrations. Hope you guys get some value from it.
